layer.label: Sinai Georgian NF M 19, Undertext (Christian Palestinian Aramaic)

text_unit.label: Dormition of the Virgin Mary B

work_wit.alt_title: Dormition of the Virgin Mary B

note.value: Both folios (4r, 4v, 5r, 5v) belong clearly to the Dormition of the Holy Virgin, but they cannot be further assigned at present, because of the fragmentary transmission of this text in the other language witnesses. It seems, however, that this passage belongs to the first part with Mary going to the Mount of Olives after the relevation by Jesus Christ. This is the third independent Christian Palestinian Aramaic version of the Dormition of the Holy Virgin (CP3)
Description by Christa Müller-Kessler
Bibliography: Müller-Kessler 2019, pp. 81-98 ; Müller-Kessler 2018b, pp. 69-95

work_wit.alt_title: Dormition of the Virgin Mary A

note.value: A second independent and previously unknown early Christian Palestinian Aramaic version of the Dormition of the Holy Virgin (CP2); partially deviating from the Syriac (S1) and Ethiopic (E1)
Description by Christa Müller-Kessler

location.repository: St. Catherine's Monastery of the Sinai

note.value: Foliation in pencil at the center or top of outer margin
Warped back, with parchment reinforcements (with writing in CPA)
Unbound codex, incomplete at beginning and end | Tightly stitched quires | Most folios are stitched together from several pieces

All quires are quaternions, except quires 1 and 8; quire 8 consists only of f. 61 | Quire signatures at the beginning of most quires | Quires are tightly stitched | Relations within the quires are difficult to establish | Only quires 1, 7, and 8 are palimpsested
